message:
Fix 15 Go backend security audit findings (#5039)

Security fixes applied:

- #5011: Move Gemini API key from URL query parameter to x-goog-api-key header
  (provider_gemini.go, server_operations.go)
- #5012: Add scanner.Err() check after bufio.Scanner loops
  (provider_geminicli.go, provider_ghcopilot.go, provider_antigravity.go)
- #5013: Add nil check before calling onChunk callback in BobProvider.StreamChat
- #5014: Sanitize AI prediction labels against allowed sets to prevent
  unbounded Prometheus cardinality (prediction_metrics.go)
- #5015: Add path traversal protection in static file serving — validate
  resolved path stays within root directory (server.go)
- #5016: Validate cron schedule format before passing to Kubernetes API
  (mcp_resources.go)
- #5017: Validate GVR parameters against Kubernetes naming conventions
  (custom_resources.go)
- #5018: Replace silent uuid.Parse error discarding with parseUUID helper
  that logs warnings for malformed UUIDs (sqlite.go)
- #5019: Use atomic file creation (link+rename) to prevent race condition
  when multiple processes create the encryption key file (crypto.go)
- #5021: Add warning logs when MCP bridge JSON parsing fails and falls
  back to empty results (bridge.go)
- #5022: Enforce 1MB max broadcast message size in WebSocket hub to
  prevent memory spikes (websocket.go)
- #5023: Add defense-in-depth LIMIT clamping (1-1000) in GetUserCardHistory
  (sqlite.go)
- #5024: Remove raw error details from card position unmarshal logs to
  prevent information disclosure (sqlite.go)
- #5025: Enforce 1MB max stdin message size in pod exec WebSocket to
  prevent memory exhaustion (exec.go)
- #5026: Validate tier field bounds (1-3) in GPU health CronJob endpoint
  (mcp_resources.go)

Fixes #5011, #5012, #5013, #5014, #5015, #5016, #5017, #5018, #5019, #5021, #5022, #5023, #5024, #5025, #5026

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -27,6 +27,32 @@ const (
 	sqliteDefaultConnMaxIdleTime = 2 * time.Minute
 )
 
+// parseUUID parses a UUID string, logging a warning if malformed instead of
+// silently returning the zero UUID. This prevents data misattribution when
+// the database contains corrupted UUID strings.
+func parseUUID(s string, field string) uuid.UUID {
+	id, err := uuid.Parse(s)
+	if err != nil {
+		slog.Warn("[Store] malformed UUID in database — using zero UUID", "field", field, "value", s, "error", err)
+	}
+	return id
+}
+
+// maxSQLLimit is the maximum allowed value for SQL LIMIT parameters.
+// This provides defense-in-depth against unbounded queries from internal callers.
+const maxSQLLimit = 1000
+
+// clampLimit ensures a SQL LIMIT parameter is within safe bounds (1 to maxSQLLimit).
+func clampLimit(limit int) int {
+	if limit < 1 {
+		return 1
+	}
+	if limit > maxSQLLimit {
+		return maxSQLLimit
+	}
+	return limit
+}
+

 func getEnvInt(key string, defaultVal int) int {
 	if v := os.Getenv(key); v != "" {
